page: Microsoft Azure M128 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 64 physical Intel Xeon 8280M cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz and utilizes the x86_64 architecture, supported by 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 38 MB L3 cache. It includes hardware flags such as AVX, AVX-512F, AES, and FMA.

The server is equipped with 2048.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per physical core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled capacity of 14,680 GB. There are no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates highly polarized performance. Single-core performance is weak, with stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip and bzip3 compression scoring in the bottom 10% to 25%. However, multi-core and memory subsystem performance are highly robust. Multi-core stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests rank in the top 25%, while large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidth reach top-tier status in the top 10% at 426.47 GB/sec and 161.78 GB/sec, respectively. Redis SET operations also perform strongly, achieving 80.9 million operations per second.

Given the massive memory capacity and high local storage, the M128 offers high resource density for memory-bound and data-intensive workloads. The primary tradeoff is the lower single-threaded efficiency. It is highly suitable for large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, software compilation, and high-throughput static web serving.
description: The Microsoft Azure M128 is a memory-optimized server from the MS family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8280M processor with 128 dedicated vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz. It features 2048.0 GB of RAM and 14,680 GB of bundled local storage, with no GPUs. While single-core benchmarks are weak, the server delivers strong multi-core performance and top-tier memory bandwidth, reaching 426.47 GB/sec for large-block uncached reads. This hardware profile provides high resource density for memory-intensive applications. The M128 is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis caching, high-throughput web serving, and parallelized workloads like software compilation, though it presents a tradeoff in single-threaded processing efficiency.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M128 is a memory-optimized server featuring 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 2048 GB RAM, and 14,680 GB local storage, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth for large-scale database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M128 is a memory-optimized server with 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 2 TB RAM, and 14.6 TB local storage, built for large-scale database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring two terabytes of RAM and massive local storage for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 2048 GB of system memory
14,680 GB of bundled local storage capacity
Top-tier memory bandwidth reaching 426.47 GB/sec for uncached reads
Strong multi-core performance for parallel compilation and ray tracing
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and high-throughput web serving
